Conversation with Gemini

You said

I am an alternative data researcher, and having an interview, the interviewer asked me what questions I would ask the vendor if i get a new datasets

Gemini said

When evaluating a new alternative dataset, the goal is to bridge the gap between "interesting information" and "tradable alpha." You want to assess the data’s reliability, its statistical integrity, and the logistical hurdles of integrating it into a production pipeline.

Here are the key questions to ask a vendor, categorized by the specific risks they address:

1. Data Integrity and Lineage

These questions help you understand if the data is "clean" and where it actually comes from.

  • What is the primary source of the data? (e.g., Is it direct-from-source, web-scraped, or aggregated from third parties?)

  • How do you handle restatements or revisions? If the vendor changes a data point post-facto, you need to know how that is flagged so it doesn’t corrupt your backtest.

  • What is the "point-in-time" availability? Ask specifically if the timestamp reflects when the event occurred or when the data was actually available to a subscriber.

2. Methodology and Pre-processing

Understanding the "black box" of the vendor’s internal processing is crucial for avoiding biased signals.

  • What outlier detection or normalization techniques are applied? You need to know if they are already smoothing the data or removing "noise" that might actually be a signal.

  • How do you handle survivorship bias? Does the dataset include companies that have gone bankrupt or been acquired, or is it only currently active tickers?

  • What is the mapping process for identifiers? Ask how they map raw data to tradable entities (e.g., mapping a brand name or a physical location to a specific FIGI or ticker).

3. Statistical and Signal Characteristics

These focus on the "alpha" potential and how the data fits into a broader portfolio.

  • What is the history and breadth of the coverage? Does the history cover multiple market cycles (e.g., 2008 or 2020), and how has the panel size evolved over time?

  • What is the data’s "decay" or update frequency? Is this intraday, daily, or monthly? This determines if it fits a high-frequency or mid-frequency strategy.

  • Is there a significant "lead time" compared to traditional data? Does this dataset lead earnings reports or economic releases, and by how much?

4. Compliance and Legal Risk

In alternative data, this is often the most important category for institutional researchers.

  • What is your framework for ensuring no Material Non-Public Information (MNPI) is included?

  • How do you ensure GDPR/CCPA compliance? (Especially relevant for consumer transaction or geolocation data).

  • Do you have exclusive rights to this data, or are you a re-seller? This impacts both the price and the risk of the signal being "crowded."

5. Technical Logistics

  • What is the delivery mechanism? (S3 bucket, Snowflake, API, or SFTP?)

  • What is the data uptime SLA? If the data is late, it can break automated trading pipelines.

Expert Tip: If you want to impress the interviewer, emphasize that you aren't just looking for "good" data, but for orthogonal data—information that isn't already correlated with the signals your firm is already trading.

Do you know if this interview is for a firm that specializes in a specific asset class, like equities or macro?

You said

can you summarize them and put into a pdf

  • Query successful

Gemini said

Python
from weasyprint import HTML

# Define the content for the PDF
html_content = """
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <style>
        @page {
            size: A4;
            margin: 20mm;
            background-color: #fcfcfc;
        }
        body {
            font-family: 'Helvetica', 'Arial', sans-serif;
            font-size: 11pt;
            line-height: 1.5;
            color: #2c3e50;
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
        }
        .header {
            background-color: #2c3e50;
            color: white;
            padding: 30px;
            text-align: center;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
        }
        h1 {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 20pt;
            letter-spacing: 1px;
        }
        .subtitle {
            font-size: 12pt;
            opacity: 0.9;
        }
        .container {
            padding: 0 10px;
        }
        h2 {
            color: #2980b9;
            font-size: 14pt;
            border-bottom: 1px solid #eee;
            padding-bottom: 5px;
            margin-top: 20px;
        }
        .category-box {
            margin-bottom: 15px;
        }
        ul {
            padding-left: 20px;
        }
        li {
            margin-bottom: 8px;
        }
        strong {
            color: #34495e;
        }
        .pro-tip {
            margin-top: 30px;
            background-color: #e8f4fd;
            border-left: 5px solid #2980b9;
            padding: 15px;
            font-style: italic;
        }
        .footer {
            position: fixed;
            bottom: 0;
            width: 100%;
            text-align: center;
            font-size: 9pt;
            color: #95a5a6;
            padding: 10px 0;
        }
    </style>
</head>
<body>
    <div class="header">
        <h1>Alternative Data Interview Prep</h1>
        <div class="subtitle">Vendor Due Diligence Framework</div>
    </div>

    <div class="container">
        <div class="category-box">
            <h2>1. Data Integrity & Lineage</h2>
            <ul>
                <li><strong>Primary Source:</strong> Is this direct-from-source, web-scraped, or aggregated?</li>
                <li><strong>Restatements:</strong> How are post-facto revisions flagged to avoid backtest corruption?</li>
                <li><strong>Point-in-Time:</strong> When was the data actually available to a subscriber vs. the event time?</li>
            </ul>
        </div>

        <div class="category-box">
            <h2>2. Methodology & Pre-processing</h2>
            <ul>
                <li><strong>Normalization:</strong> What outlier detection or smoothing techniques are applied internally?</li>
                <li><strong>Survivorship Bias:</strong> Are bankrupt or acquired entities included in the history?</li>
                <li><strong>Mapping:</strong> How is raw data linked to tradable identifiers like Tickers or FIGIs?</li>
            </ul>
        </div>

        <div class="category-box">
            <h2>3. Statistical & Signal Characteristics</h2>
            <ul>
                <li><strong>History/Breadth:</strong> Does it cover multiple market cycles (e.g., 2008, 2020)?</li>
                <li><strong>Decay:</strong> What is the update frequency (Daily vs. Monthly) and signal decay speed?</li>
                <li><strong>Lead Time:</strong> How much of a lead does this have over traditional financial reports?</li>
            </ul>
        </div>

        <div class="category-box">
            <h2>4. Compliance & Legal Risk</h2>
            <ul>
                <li><strong>MNPI:</strong> What are the specific controls to ensure no Material Non-Public Information is shared?</li>
                <li><strong>Privacy:</strong> Is the data fully GDPR/CCPA compliant for consumer-level data?</li>
                <li><strong>Rights:</strong> Is the vendor the primary owner or a reseller?</li>
            </ul>
        </div>

        <div class="category-box">
            <h2>5. Technical Logistics</h2>
            <ul>
                <li><strong>Delivery:</strong> Mechanism (S3, API, Snowflake) and data format.</li>
                <li><strong>SLA:</strong> Uptime guarantees and latency protocols for production pipelines.</li>
            </ul>
        </div>

        <div class="pro-tip">
            <strong>Pro-Tip:</strong> Emphasize "Orthogonality." Interviewers look for researchers who prioritize data that provides a unique signal not already captured by existing models.
        </div>
    </div>

    <div class="footer">
        Alternative Data Research - Vendor Evaluation Guide
    </div>
</body>
</html>
"""

# Generate the PDF
output_pdf_path = "Alt_Data_Vendor_Interview_Guide.pdf"
HTML(string=html_content).write_pdf(output_pdf_path)
print(output_pdf_path)
Code output
Alt_Data_Vendor_Interview_Guide.pdf

Your PDF summary for the alternative data vendor evaluation framework is ready.

Alt_Data_V...view_Guide
PDF

This document covers the five critical pillars of vendor due diligence:

  • Data Integrity & Lineage: Ensuring the "source of truth."

  • Methodology: Understanding internal cleaning and mapping.

  • Statistical Characteristics: Assessing alpha decay and history.

  • Compliance: Managing MNPI and privacy risks.

  • Logistics: Technical delivery and production reliability.

Good luck with your interview!
